title: Manipulation of electromagnetic wave propagation in quantum-spin-chain medium
authors: Taras Krokhmalskii, Taras Verkholyak, Ostap Baran, Dmytro Yaremchuk, Taras Hutak, Oleg Derzhko
abstract: We consider a simple model of one-dimensional magnetic crystal and examine the propagation of an electromagnetic wave through such a medium. Calculating the dispersion relation ${\bf k}(ω)$ allows us to illustrate how the spread of the electromagnetic wave can be controlled by an external magnetic field. Our rigorous calculations should be useful for more realistic (and less tractable mathematically) models of magnetic media.
summary: This paper explores how an external magnetic field can manipulate the propagation of electromagnetic waves through a quantum spin chain. By calculating the dispersion relation, the authors show that the wave velocity and attenuation can be precisely controlled, especially in the THz frequency range. The findings provide a theoretical foundation for using quantum magnetic media as tunable components for electromagnetic wave control.
main_problem: Investigating how the propagation, velocity, and attenuation of electromagnetic waves can be controlled in a one-dimensional magnetic crystal using an external magnetic field.
main_result: The study identifies that the dispersion relation and refractive index are highly tunable via the magnetic field, revealing specific frequency regimes of anomalous dispersion and damping, particularly at characteristic frequencies related to the field strength.
method: The authors use Jordan-Wigner fermionization to solve the spin Hamiltonian exactly and apply Maxwell's equations in matter to derive the electromagnetic dispersion relation.
model_or_system: A 1D spin-1/2 isotropic XY chain in a transverse magnetic field, representing a quasi-1D magnetic crystal with nearest-neighbor exchange coupling J.
key_observables: Complex wavevector k(omega), dispersion relation, magnetic susceptibility tensor, refractive index, wave velocity, and attenuation coefficient.
important_parameters: Magnetic field strength (B/J), frequency (omega/J), temperature (T), and exchange interaction (J).
main_assumptions: The medium is assumed to be nonconducting and nonpolarizable; the interatomic distance is much smaller than the wavelength; the model is limited to an exactly solvable free-fermion system.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of EM wave propagation and field orientations; Figure 2 displays the dynamic susceptibility; Figure 3 plots wave velocity and damping across different magnetic field strengths.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical model, establishes the mathematical framework for EM propagation in magnetic media, performs exact calculations using fermionization, analyzes results in strong and weak field regimes, and discusses future generalizations.
why_interesting: This work is relevant to quantum optics and many-body dynamics as it demonstrates a mechanism for controlling light-matter interaction and wave propagation using tunable quantum many-body states.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic drawing of electromagnetic wave prop- agation in quantum-spin-chain medium. An electromagnetic wave propagates along the x direction, i.e., k=(kx, 0, 0). Case 1: Ekω=(0, Ey, 0), Bkω=(0, 0, Bz). Case 2: Ekω=(0, 0, Ez), Bkω=(0, By, 0).

Figure 2. χ′ xx(κ = 0, ω) (solid) and χ′′ xx(κ = 0, ω) (dashed) for the spin-1/2 isotropic XY chain in a transverse field. J = 1, B incorporates m0, T = 0.02. From top to bottom: B = 0.1, 0.5, 0.9, 1, and 2.

Figure 3. Towards the dispersion relation k(ω), see Eq. (14): ck′(ω)/ω (solid) and ck′′(ω)/ω (dashed) for the spin-1/2 isotropic XY chain in a transverse field. J = 1, B incor- porates m0, and T = 0.02. From top to bottom: B = 0.1, 0.5, 0.9, 1, and 2. The insets present the same data as in the main panels but for higher frequencies.

Spintronics.note: The paper directly addresses the interface of optics and magnetism by deriving the electromagnetic dispersion relation and refractive index within a magnetic crystal system.
